headline: Subsidy rationalisation saved RM14 billion but delivery mechanism leaks 30%
context: Malaysia's 2024-2025 subsidy rationalisation saved RM14 billion on paper, but implementation audits show 30% of targeted subsidies still reached non-eligible recipients.

cards.big: Subsidy reform saved RM14 billion on paper — but 30% still went to wrong people
cards.sub: Implementation failure means RM4.2 billion in subsidies missed their intended targets

cards.big: PADU database used for targeting has 2.8 million incomplete or outdated records
cards.sub: Targeting system's data quality undermines the entire rationalisation exercise

cards.big: RM4.2 billion leakage equals the combined allocation for rural roads and bridges
cards.sub: Subsidy misdirection costs as much as major infrastructure programmes

cards.big: No independent body audits subsidy delivery — ministries self-report effectiveness
cards.sub: Agencies implementing subsidies also evaluate whether they're working

cards.big: Subsidy reform that leaks 30% hasn't reformed the subsidy — it's reformed who complains about it
Should subsidy delivery be independently audited with results published quarterly?
